LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JONATHAN FROMMER AND JESSICA NALL'S MOTION FOR AWARD OF ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 - THE COURT AWARDS $25,000 AS A REASONABLE FEE FOR PREVAILING ON THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION AND PREPARING THE INSTANT FEE MOTION. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT'S ANTI-SLAPP MOTION WAS DENIED IN PART, THE MOTION ONLY ADDRESSED PART OF PLAINTIFF'S ACTION, AND THERE APPEARS TO BE SOME OVERSTAFFING/DUPLICATIVE WORK. DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nell Clement of Farella Braun & Martel, LLP telephone number 415-954-4400 for Defendants.
No other appearance was made on this matter.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Palintff and CROSS DEFENDANT WILSON YUE'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OF DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS FROMMER AND NALL is overruled in its entirety. Plaintiff fails to show that the claims are time barred or that the statute of repose applies. Defendant alleges that the wrongful conduct is ongoing (Pars. 8 and 9 of the Cross-Complaint). In addition, the allegations at par. 6 of the cross-complaint indicate that the discovery rule may apply and defendant discovered the wrongdoing in 2013. The cross-complaint is not uncertain and defendant pleads sufficient facts regarding the harm/damages at pars. 6, 7, and 56. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint By Defendants Frommer And Nall
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nell Clement of Farella Braun & Martel, LLP telephone number 415-954-4400 for Defendants.
No other appearance was made on this matter.

The Court adopted its tentitive ruling. PLAINTIFF WILSON YUE'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint By Defendants Frommer And Nall is granted with leave to amend to plead a basis to recover attorney's fees, e.g. CCP 1021.5. Whether defendant will ultimately recover those fees and whether any legal victory will transcend defendant's personal interest in the case is a factual issue that is better resolved later in the litigation. See Summit Media LLC v. City of Los Angeles (2015) 240 Cal.App.4th 171, 188. The court denies the motion as to the claim for punitive damages. Defendant sufficiently alleges malicious conduct. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nell Clement of Farella Braun & Martel, LLP telephone number 415-954-4400 for Defendants.
No other appearance was made on this matter.

Ruling: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JONATHAN FROMMER and JESSICA NALL'S Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 - The court awards $25,000 as a reasonable fee for prevailing on the anti-SLAPP motion and preparing the instant fee motion. The court notes that defendant's anti-SLAPP motion was denied in part, the motion only addressed part of plaintiff's action, and there appears to be some overstaffing/duplicative work. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489. (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2015 Continued To Nov-17-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Cross-Complaint By Defendants Frommer And Nall
Off Calendar Aug-27-2015 Continued To Nov-17-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Aug-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. MICHAEL CARROLL, TEL. NO. 1-415-788-7600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. NELL K. CLEMENT OF FARELLA BRAUN MARTEL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-954-4400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JONATHAN FROMMER AND JESSICA NALL'S Special Motion To Strike is Granted as to Causes of Action Five and Six. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity'If the court finds such a showing has been made'it then determines whether the plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) The first prong is met if the moving party meets the burden of making a prima facie showing that the plaintiff's cause of action arises out of an act in furtherance of a person's right to petition or free speech under the US or CA Const. in connection with a public issue. (CCP 425.16(b)(1).) The first prong is met here, as these Causes of Action involve claims that the Defendants obtained building permits by false pretenses and abused the San Francisco construction permitting process. These claims are neither incidental to these Causes of Action, nor concededly or clearly illegal activity. (See Wang v. Wal-Mart Real Estate Business Trust (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 790, 802; Flatley v. Mauro (2006) 39 Cal.4th 299, 320.) Plaintiff has not demonstrated a probability of prevailing. Cause of Action 5, for violation of Business & Professions Code section 17200, is implicated by "anything that can properly be called a business practice and that at the same time is forbidden by law." (Barquis v. Merchants Collection Assn. (1972) 7 Cal.3d 94, 113.) Here, no competent, admissible evidence is presented to show that these Defendants engaged in any sort of business practice at all; they are merely owners of the renovated house. Cause of Action 6, for violation of California Government Code section 36900, requires proof of violation of a City Ordinance. Here, the admissible evidence, the Declaration of John Richards, amounts to nothing more than improper expert testimony as to legal conclusions. (See Summer v. A. L. Gilbert Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 1155, 1178.) The Court sustains Defendants' objections to the other declarations. Denied as to Cause of Action Eight. This Cause of Action, for declaratory relief, does not sufficiently implicate a protected activity. Although the Cause of Action does purport to incorporate by reference all previous allegations in the complaint, because the Court hereby strikes Causes of Action Five and Six, the remaining allegations amount to at most merely incidental references to protected activity. (See Wang, supra, 153 Cal.App.4th at 802.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
